title: HV-CTAB-PCI DNA Extraction Protocol
author: Vicky Ooi
materials: Lysis Buffer 1 (LB1: CTAB 2 %, Tris– HCL 100 mM, EDTA 20 mM, NaCl 1 M)
Lysis Buffer 2 (LB2: CTAB 10 %, NaCl 0.5 M, pH 5.5)

steps.title: Clean the working bench with TriGene disinfectant
steps.instruction: Clean the working bench with TriGene disinfectant.

steps.title: Scrape 1 g of faecal material...
steps.instruction: Scrape 1 g of faecal material from the outer surface of a faeces and put it into a 15 mL centrifuge tube. 1 g, of faeces

steps.title: Transfer the faecal material into a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the faecal material into a mortar and grind the faeces into powder with liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of Lysis Buffer 1 (LB1
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of Lysis Buffer 1 (LB1: CTAB 2 %, Tris– HCL 100 mM, EDTA 20 mM, NaCl 1.4 M, pH 7.5) to the mortar containing the faecal material to further grind and mix in the buffer with the ground faeces. Transfer the liquid back into the 15 mL tube. 1 mL, of Lysis Buffer 1 (LB1)

steps.title: Add another 1 mL of LB1...
steps.instruction: Add another 1 mL of LB1 to the mortar to mix in any leftover faecal material on the mortar and transfer the liquid back into the 15 mL tube. Repeat this step once more, and then add 2 mL of LB1 to the 15 mL tube containing the faecal homogenate. Thus, a total of 5 mL of LB1 would be added to the ground faeces. 1 mL, of LB1 + 1 mL, LB1 + 2 mL, LB1

steps.title: Vortex the faecal homogenate and incubate...
steps.instruction: Vortex the faecal homogenate and incubate in a thermomixer for 3 h, with occasional mixing, at 60°C for cell lysis. 03:00:00, of incubation at 60 °C

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 3,150 g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 3,150 g (4,000 rpm) for 12 min and pipette 4 mL of the supernatant into a new 15 mL tube. 00:12:00, of centrifugation 4 mL, of supernatant

steps.title: Add 4 mL of phenol
steps.instruction: Add 4 mL of phenol: chloroform: isoamyl alcohol (21:20:1) to the supernatant, then gently mix the tube. Centrifuge the sample for 3 min at 3,150 g (4,000 rpm) and pipette 3 mL of the aqueous phase into a new 15 mL tube. 4 mL, of phenol: chloroform: isoamyl alcohol 00:03:00, of centrifugation 3 mL, of aqueous phase

steps.title: Add 330 µL of Lysis Buffer 2 (LB2
steps.instruction: Add 330 µL of Lysis Buffer 2 (LB2: CTAB 10 %, NaCl 0.5 M, pH 5.5) to the aqueous phase, and leave it to lyse further at 60°C for 4 h. 330 µL, of Lysis Buffer 2 04:00:00, of incubation at 60 °C

steps.title: Add 104 µL of protease to...
steps.instruction: Add 104 µL of protease to the sample and leave it to digest proteins at 60°C for another 1 h. 104 µL, of protease 01:00:00, of incubation at 60 °C

steps.title: Add 3434 µL of phenol
steps.instruction: Add 3434 µL of phenol: chloroform: isoamyl alcohol (21:20:1) to the mixture, and then gently mix the tube well, and centrifuge the sample for 12 min at 3,150 g (4,000 rpm). Then, pipette 3 mL of the aqueous phase into a new 15 mL tube. 3434 µL, of phenol: chloroform: isoamyl alcohol 00:12:00, of centrifugation 3 mL, of aqueous phase

steps.title: Add one volume of isopropanol (i
steps.instruction: Add one volume of isopropanol (i.e., 3 mL) to precipitate the DNA overnight at -20°C. 3 mL, of isopropanol -20 °C Overnight

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample for 20 min...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample for 20 min at 8000 g (5,200 rpm), and then get rid of all the supernatant. 00:20:00, of centrifugation

steps.title: Add 400 µL of 70% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL of 70% ethanol to the pellet to wash it. Vortex and then centrifuge the sample at 3,150 g for 12 min and get rid of the supernatant. 400 µL, of 70 % ethanol 00:12:00, of centrifugation

steps.title: Dry the pellet in a fume...
steps.instruction: Dry the pellet in a fume hood at room temperature for 15 min. 00:15:00 at Room temperature

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 250 µL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 250 µL of TE buffer (10 mM Tris–HCl, 1 mM EDTA, pH 8). 250 µL, of TE buffer

steps.title: Store the DNA isolate at -20°C...
steps.instruction: Store the DNA isolate at -20°C for use within a week or at -80°C for longer-time storage. -20 °C -80 °C
